rationale: Antia et al. is cited repeatedly in functional roles, not only as generic background: it is included in the focal comparison table, used to motivate why governance alignment matters, and referenced in hypothesis logic about moral hazard and enforcement. The citing paper explicitly positions its contribution as advancing beyond prior dual-distribution/franchising evidence toward a broader B2B multichannel design framework with financial performance outcomes. This use indicates a meaningful intellectual link rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Antia et al. (2017) as a key prior study showing that governance alignment in dual distribution affects channel outcomes, then broadens that line of work. It extends the prior logic to B2B multichannel systems by modeling direct versus indirect channel usage and testing their interactions with governance mechanisms on manufacturer EBIT.
